codeReview.summary: The feature adds substantial multi-device Playwright coverage, but two requirement-critical gaps remain: asymmetric ±120s peer skew is not tested, and the long-session loop can pass even when most periodic sign/ECDH cycles fail.

issues.description: The spec does not verify the required asymmetric case "device B ±120s clock vs A". It hardcodes asymmetric skew to ±25s (`ASYMMETRIC_SKEW_MS = 25_000`) and only runs ±120s in symmetric mode (both devices shifted equally), so the feature's stated behavior is unproven.

Periodic-activity success criteria are too weak: the test only requires `successfulSigns >= 1` and `successfulEcdhs >= 1` across all iterations. This allows most sign/ECDH cycles to fail while still passing, which does not adequately validate stability for a sustained periodic session.
Heap-leak assertion samples only page A baseline/final (`baselineA`/`finalA`) and does not evaluate page B memory. This leaves a blind spot for leaks that manifest only on the responder device.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-worker procedure requires manual agent-browser verification for fulfilled assertions, but this worker transcript shows only `mission-worker-base` and `runtime-worker` skills and no `agent-browser` invocation while handoff still reports `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: runtime-worker SKILL.md lines 49-50 require agent-browser checks; transcript entry for workerSessionId ed09be73-668f-40b5-9d2b-790a7c656eb3 (worker-transcripts.jsonl line 119) lists tool flow without agent-browser skill calls.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Key constraints discovered here (bifrost `max_future_skew_secs=30` impact on skew tests and duration-compressed long-session strategy) are documented in `docs/runtime-deviations-from-paper.md` but not in `.factory/library`, so future workers may re-discover the same constraints during planning.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: New deviation entries exist in docs/runtime-deviations-from-paper.md around lines 790 and 846, while grep over `.factory/library/*` finds no matches for `max_future_skew_secs`, `clock skew`, or `LONG_SESSION_ITERATIONS`.

summary: Review failed: the implementation provides useful tests but does not fully satisfy the feature's asymmetric clock-skew requirement and uses permissive long-session success thresholds that can mask repeated operation failures.
